last_digest.title: U.S. Targets Russian Cyber Spies With $10M Bounty Over Messaging App Attacks
last_digest.rss_summary: The U.S. offers up to $10M for information on Russian hackers targeting Signal and WhatsApp accounts of officials and journalists. The U.S. government is offering rewards of up to $10 million for information leading to the identification of members of the Russian-linked groups UNC5792 and UNC4221. The hackers target government officials, military personnel, journalists, and […]

last_digest.title: Aflac Japan Data Breach Impacts 4.38 Million
last_digest.rss_summary:
Hackers accessed the insurance giant’s policyholder portal multiple times between June 15 and June 25.
The post Aflac Japan Data Breach Impacts 4.38 Million appeared first on SecurityWeek.

last_digest.title: CISA: Windows BlueHammer flaw now exploited by ransomware gangs
last_digest.rss_summary: CISA confirmed on Monday that ransomware gangs are now exploiting a Microsoft Defender privilege escalation vulnerability, dubbed BlueHammer, that has previously been abused in zero-day attacks. [...]

last_digest.title: Nissan Discloses Employee Data Breach Linked to Oracle Zero-Day
last_digest.rss_summary: Nissan says employees' data was stolen via the Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day campaign

last_digest.title: Phantom Squatting: AI-Hallucinated Domains as a Software Supply Chain Vector
last_digest.rss_summary:
Attackers can exploit LLM domain hallucinations through phantom squatting to target supply chains. Read the analysis to learn more. 
The post Phantom Squatting: AI-Hallucinated Domains as a Software Supply Chain Vector appeared first on Unit 42.

last_week_digest.title: WhatsApp phishing attack uses fake business docs to hack PCs
last_week_digest.tldr: A global WhatsApp phishing campaign is compromising business accounts to distribute malicious VBScript files, granting attackers remote administrative access. Spanning twelve countries, this sophisticated operation signals a growing trend of leveraging trusted communication channels to bypass security, forcing organisations to re-evaluate internal verification protocols.

last_week_digest.title: ShapedPlugin Supply Chain Attack Backdoors Pro Plugin Updates
last_week_digest.tldr: Attackers compromised ShapedPlugin’s build pipeline, injecting backdoors into three Pro WordPress plugins. This supply chain attack, affecting hundreds of thousands of sites, exfiltrated credentials and 2FA secrets. It signals a growing industry trend where trusted software update channels are weaponised to bypass standard security defences.

last_week_digest.title: Phishing hides in routine Microsoft 365 workflows
last_week_digest.tldr: Attackers are exploiting Microsoft 365 collaboration features, including Outlook Groups and calendar invites, to bypass traditional security filters. By embedding phishing lures within trusted productivity workflows, threat actors increase credential theft risks, signalling that organisations must now treat internal collaborative tools as high-risk attack vectors.

last_week_digest.title: What the Latest ShinyHunters Breaches Reveal About Modern Cyberattacks
last_week_digest.tldr: ShinyHunters’ recent breaches, exposing millions of user records, demonstrate that attackers increasingly bypass complex malware to exploit systemic vulnerabilities. These incidents disrupt global operations and erode consumer trust, signalling a critical industry shift towards prioritising robust identity management over traditional perimeter-based security defences.

last_week_digest.title: Canadian utility fesses up to data breach, but key details remain off-grid
last_week_digest.tldr: London Hydro is investigating a security breach potentially exposing the personal data of over 160,000 customers. While financial details remain secure, the incident highlights how utility providers face increasing risks, signalling that critical infrastructure firms must prioritise data transparency to maintain public trust.

last_week_digest.title: Xsolis Data Breach Impacts 1.4 Million People
last_week_digest.tldr: Healthcare technology firm Xsolis recently suffered a data breach after a phishing attack exposed the sensitive personal and medical information of 1.4 million individuals. This incident disrupts client operations and highlights the critical vulnerability of third-party service providers, signalling an urgent need for enhanced supply chain security across global industries.

last_week_digest.title: LastPass confirms data breach in Klue supply chain attack
last_week_digest.tldr: LastPass suffered a data breach after the Icarus group exploited a Klue supply chain vulnerability to steal OAuth tokens. Attackers accessed Salesforce CRM data, exposing customer contact and support information. This incident highlights the critical operational risks posed by third-party integrations, signalling a growing industry need for rigorous vendor security.

last_week_digest.title: FortiBleed Attackers Turn Firewalls Into Credential Stealers as Heists Persist
last_week_digest.tldr: The FortiBleed campaign has compromised 430,000 FortiGate firewalls globally, enabling attackers to harvest 110 million credentials using a custom sniffer tool. This breach of high-value defense contractors signals a shift toward weaponising perimeter infrastructure, forcing organisations to recognise that compromised network hardware now serves as a persistent, scalable threat vector.

last_week_digest.title: The Global Namespace Risk: Universal Bucket Hijacking Technique for Cloud Data Exfiltration
last_week_digest.tldr: Researchers have identified a critical architectural flaw affecting major cloud providers, where attackers can hijack globally unique storage buckets to reroute sensitive data streams. This vulnerability poses significant operational risks, potentially enabling silent exfiltration of logs. It signals a shift toward prioritizing immutable identity verification over predictable, name-based cloud infrastructure configurations.

last_week_digest.title: Data Exposure Flaws Threaten Dify AI Platform Used by 1 Million Apps
last_week_digest.tldr: Critical vulnerabilities in the Dify AI platform have exposed sensitive data across one million applications, allowing unauthorised access to private chats, internal APIs, and tenant documents. This incident underscores the systemic risks inherent in multi-tenant AI infrastructure, highlighting how platform-level flaws can compromise global business operations and undermine enterprise data security.

last_week_digest.title: Iran-Linked MuddyWater Poses as Ransomware Gang to Mask Cyber Espionage
last_week_digest.tldr: The Iranian-linked MuddyWater group is masking espionage as ransomware attacks by mimicking Chaos gang tactics, including extortion notes. This convergence of nation-state activity and cybercrime complicates incident response, forcing global organisations to prioritise behavioural analysis over traditional signature-based detection to identify true adversary objectives.

last_week_digest.title: Malicious hackers exploit Cisco zero-day for highest access level at communications service provider
last_week_digest.tldr: Hackers exploited a zero-day vulnerability in Cisco SD-WAN software to gain root-level access at a communications service provider. This breach allowed undetected surveillance of internal traffic, signalling a critical industry shift where attackers increasingly target edge devices to bypass traditional security perimeters globally.

last_week_digest.title: CISA warns of max severity Ubiquiti flaws exploited in attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: CISA has issued an urgent directive following active exploitation of critical vulnerabilities in Ubiquiti UniFi OS and Lantronix servers. These flaws allow remote code execution, threatening global network integrity. This trend signals that infrastructure providers remain primary targets, necessitating immediate patching to prevent widespread compromise.

last_week_digest.title: Indian auto giant Bajaj Auto hit by ransomware incident
last_week_digest.tldr: Indian automotive leader Bajaj Auto recently suffered a ransomware attack impacting its core operations and technology subsidiary. This incident, following a similar breach at Tata Electronics, highlights a growing trend of cyber extortion targeting major manufacturing firms, signalling increased systemic risk for global industrial supply chains.

last_week_digest.title: Exclusive: Meet AIVEX, a New Triage Model Built to Reduce Supply Chain Threat and Risk
last_week_digest.tldr: The introduction of the AIVEX framework addresses critical software supply chain vulnerabilities within AI-driven environments. By prioritising operational and business risks, this model signals a shift toward proactive risk management, helping global organisations mitigate complex threats that could otherwise disrupt essential digital infrastructure and services.

last_week_digest.title: Russian APT Deploys ‘StockStay’ Backdoor Against Ukrainian Targets
last_week_digest.tldr: The Russian-linked threat group Turla is actively deploying the StockStay backdoor to compromise critical government and military organisations across Ukraine. This persistent espionage campaign highlights the escalating risk of state-sponsored cyber warfare, signalling a broader industry shift where advanced persistent threats increasingly leverage bespoke, stealthy malware to facilitate long-term intelligence gathering against sensitive global infrastructure.

last_week_digest.title: Poland busts SIM-swapping gang tied to millions in crypto theft
last_week_digest.tldr: Polish authorities arrested four individuals for orchestrating a sophisticated SIM-swapping operation that laundered over $5 million in stolen cryptocurrency. By compromising telecommunications infrastructure and employee email accounts, the gang hijacked identities to bypass security protocols. This case signals a growing trend of organized cybercrime syndicates targeting interconnected digital ecosystems to facilitate large-scale financial theft.

last_week_digest.title: Tata Electronics Confirms Data Breach After 630GB Leak Claim Targets Apple and Tesla
last_week_digest.tldr: Tata Electronics confirmed a cybersecurity breach after the WorldLeaks group claimed to steal 630GB of data, including sensitive Apple and Tesla manufacturing documents across 204,300 files. While operations remain stable, the incident highlights how extortion-focused gangs are increasingly targeting global supply chain partners, signalling a shift toward data theft over traditional ransomware encryption tactics.

last_week_digest.title: Inside Mistic, the New Stealth Backdoor in Ransomware Intrusions
last_week_digest.tldr: KongTuke-linked actors are deploying the Mistic backdoor to maintain long-term, stealthy access within insurance, education, and IT firms globally. By sideloading malicious DLLs into legitimate Microsoft processes, attackers facilitate ransomware deployment by groups like Black Basta. This trend signals a shift toward sophisticated, fileless persistence, challenging traditional endpoint detection and increasing operational risks.

last_week_digest.title: CL-STA-1062 Targets Southeast Asian Governments and Critical Infrastructure
last_week_digest.tldr: The threat actor CL-STA-1062 has compromised at least ten organisations across Southeast Asia since 2025, specifically targeting government entities and critical energy infrastructure. By deploying bespoke backdoors like TinyRCT, attackers exfiltrated sensitive database information and source code. This sustained campaign signals a sophisticated, long-term regional strategy focused on espionage and lateral network movement.

last_week_digest.title: KDDI Data Breach Impacts up to 14.2 Million Email Accounts at Six ISPs
last_week_digest.tldr: Japanese telecommunications giant KDDI recently suffered a significant data breach, exposing up to 14.2 million email accounts across six internet service providers. This incident, triggered by a third-party software vulnerability, highlights the critical operational risks inherent in complex supply chains, signalling that even major infrastructure providers remain highly susceptible to widespread, systemic cyber threats.

last_week_digest.title: Ransomware gangs find Europe’s weakest link in third-party suppliers
last_week_digest.tldr: Ransomware incidents across Europe surged 55.1% in early 2026, with third-party suppliers emerging as critical vulnerabilities. Targeting sectors like manufacturing and IT, attackers now exploit interconnected supply chains to maximise downstream disruption. This trend forces organisations to adopt stringent risk management under NIS2 and DORA regulations, signalling a shift toward mandatory, comprehensive ecosystem oversight.

last_week_digest.title: SECURITY AFFAIRS MALWARE NEWSLETTER ROUND 103
last_week_digest.tldr: A massive data breach at KDDI has compromised up to 14.2 million email accounts across six internet service providers, highlighting severe vulnerabilities in global telecommunications infrastructure. This incident, alongside widespread supply chain compromises and router botnet activities, signals an escalating trend where attackers increasingly target critical service providers to facilitate large-scale, automated cyber operations.

last_week_digest.title: Turla group adds more malware to Russia’s espionage efforts against Ukraine
last_week_digest.tldr: Russian state-backed group Turla has deployed a sophisticated malware strain, StockStay, to target Ukrainian government and military organisations since December 2022. By masquerading as legitimate software, the attackers gained persistent access across Europe. This ongoing campaign signals a strategic shift towards building redundant, parallel malware ecosystems, complicating detection efforts for global security professionals.

last_week_digest.title: $3 Million Reportedly Stolen in Polymarket Hack
last_week_digest.tldr: Polymarket recently suffered a security breach after hackers compromised a third-party vendor, resulting in the theft of approximately $3 million from user accounts. This incident highlights the critical operational risks posed by supply chain vulnerabilities in decentralised finance, signalling an urgent need for organisations to enhance third-party oversight to protect global digital assets effectively.

last_week_digest.title: Nissan discloses employee data breach linked to Oracle zero-day attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: Nissan confirmed a data breach affecting employees across the Americas after attackers exploited an Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day vulnerability. This incident, linked to the ShinyHunters group, highlights the significant risks posed by third-party software vulnerabilities, signalling an urgent need for robust supply chain security.

last_week_digest.title: SSU and FBI Uncover Russian Cyber Espionage Operation Against Officials and Military Personnel
last_week_digest.tldr: The SSU and FBI exposed a sustained Russian intelligence campaign targeting government and military messaging accounts globally. By stealing recovery keys and credentials, attackers gain persistent access to sensitive data, signalling a shift toward mass-scale social engineering that threatens the operational security of international organisations.

last_week_digest.title: NAIC says public data stolen in ShinyHunters' PeopleSoft breach
last_week_digest.tldr: The National Association of Insurance Commissioners suffered a breach via an Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day, causing operational disruptions and temporary data feed suspensions. While ShinyHunters claimed massive theft, the incident signals how extortion groups leverage exaggerated claims to pressure organisations, highlighting critical supply chain vulnerabilities.

last_week_digest.title: US posts $10 million reward over Russian cyber campaign targeting Signal, WhatsApp
last_week_digest.tldr: The US has offered a $10 million reward to identify Russian hackers targeting Signal and WhatsApp accounts of global officials. By stealing backup recovery keys, these state-linked groups compromise sensitive communications, signalling a shift toward social engineering as a primary threat to encrypted business intelligence.

last_week_digest.title: Insurance Regulators Group NAIC Hit in Oracle PeopleSoft Hack
last_week_digest.tldr: The ShinyHunters extortion group has compromised the National Association of Insurance Commissioners, exfiltrating 3.1 terabytes of sensitive data from their Oracle PeopleSoft environment. This breach highlights critical vulnerabilities in legacy enterprise software, signalling an urgent need for organisations to prioritise rigorous third-party security assessments.

last_week_digest.title: U.S. Targets Russian Cyber Spies With $10M Bounty Over Messaging App Attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: The United States has issued a $10 million bounty for information on Russian-linked groups UNC5792 and UNC4221, which compromised thousands of Signal and WhatsApp accounts belonging to global officials and journalists. This escalation signals a shift toward state-sponsored social engineering, highlighting critical vulnerabilities in secure messaging platforms for international organisations.

last_week_digest.title: Aflac Japan Data Breach Impacts 4.38 Million
last_week_digest.tldr: Aflac Japan recently confirmed a significant data breach involving unauthorised access to its policyholder portal, exposing the personal information of 4.38 million customers between June 15 and June 25. This incident disrupts operational trust and highlights the persistent vulnerability of insurance giants to sophisticated cyberattacks targeting sensitive global consumer databases.

last_week_digest.title: CISA: Windows BlueHammer flaw now exploited by ransomware gangs
last_week_digest.tldr: Ransomware gangs are actively exploiting the BlueHammer privilege escalation vulnerability in Microsoft Defender, allowing attackers to seize SYSTEM-level control of compromised Windows devices. This escalation, following public leak of exploit code, signals a growing industry trend where disclosed zero-day flaws are rapidly weaponised, forcing organisations to accelerate patching cycles globally.

last_week_digest.title: Nissan Discloses Employee Data Breach Linked to Oracle Zero-Day
last_week_digest.tldr: Nissan confirmed that hackers exploited an Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day, exposing sensitive personal and financial data of employees across the Americas. This breach, part of a wider campaign targeting over 100 organisations, signals a growing industry vulnerability where enterprise software flaws facilitate mass-casualty data theft, forcing firms to implement restrictive payroll security.

last_week_digest.title: Phantom Squatting: AI-Hallucinated Domains as a Software Supply Chain Vector
last_week_digest.tldr: Researchers identified phantom squatting, where adversaries register AI-hallucinated domains to intercept traffic from automated systems. Analyzing 913 global brands, they discovered 13,229 malicious URLs and 250,000 unregistered targets. This emerging supply chain vector signals that developers must now verify all AI-generated links to prevent data exfiltration and secure enterprise CI/CD pipelines.
